revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Asukabe-jinja]][[ja:飛鳥戸神社]] {{Expand Japanese|飛鳥戸神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Asukabe Shrine | native_name = 飛鳥戸神社 | image = Asukabe-jinja haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Asukabe Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.535503|135.638889}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|ar|سوسانو|azb|سوسانو|bcl|Susanoo|be|Сусанаа|bg|Сусаноо|ca|Susanoo|cs|Susanoo|da|Susa-no-o|de|Susanoo|el|Σουσάνoο|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|eo|Susanoo|es|Susanoo|eu|Susanoo|fa|سوسانو (خدا)|fi|Susanoo|fr|Susanoo|hr|Susano|hu|Szuszanoo|hy|Սուսանո|id|Susanoo|it|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|ka|სუსანოო-ნო-მიკოტო|ko|스사노오|la|Susano'o|lb|Susanoo|mni|ꯁꯨꯁꯅꯨ-ꯅꯣ-ꯃꯤꯀꯣꯇꯣ|nl|Susanoo|pl|Susanoo|pt|Susanoo|ro|Susanoo|ru|Сусаноо|simple|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|sl|Susanovo|sr|Сусаноо|su|Susanoo|sv|Susanoo|ta|சுசானவோ|th|ซูซาโนโอะ|tr|Susanoo|uk|Сусаноо|vi|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|zh_min_nan|Susanoo|zh|素戔嗚尊|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} }} {{nihongo|'''Asukabe Shrine'''|飛鳥戸神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q9610964|ltq=Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} * {{ill|Q135009132|ltq=Q135009132|qid=Q135009132|1=Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings}} * {{ill|Kanpei-sha|lt=Kanpei-sha|simple|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.535503|135.638889}} == image (P18) == * Asukabe-jinja haiden.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * (خدا)|fi|Susanoo|fr|Susanoo|hr|Susano|hu|Szuszanoo|hy|Սուսանո|id|Susanoo|it|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|ka|სუსანოო-ნო-მიკოტო|ko|스사노오|la|Susano'o|lb|Susanoo|mni|ꯁꯨꯁꯅꯨ-ꯅꯣ-ꯃꯤꯀꯣꯇꯣ|nl|Susanoo|pl|Susanoo|pt|Susanoo|ro|Susanoo|ru|Сусаноо|simple|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|sl|Susanovo|sr|Сусаноо|su|Susanoo|sv|Susanoo|ta|சுசானவோ|th|ซูซาโนโอะ|tr|Susanoo|uk|Сусаноо|vi|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|zh_min_nan|Susanoo|zh|素戔嗚尊|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|ltq=Q135037878|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20041124015104/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=31201 == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 180888 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917284|ltq=Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11504610|ltq=Q11504610|qid=Q11504610|1=Unranked}} * {{ill|Q11123338|ltq=Q11123338|qid=Q11123338|1=Senior Fourth Rank}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8120105005008 == Commons category (P373) == * Asukabe-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府羽曳野市飛鳥1023', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Asukabe-jinja, entrance.jpg {{commons category|Category:Asukabe-jinja}} [[JA:飛鳥戸神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11665970}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=April 2020}} {{Shrine | Name=Asukabe Shrine | Image=[[File:Asukabe-jinja, haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall | Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Habikino City|Q694308|qid=Q694308|1=Habikino}}Asuka 1023 | Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Grand Shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|qid=Q2962|1=October 17}} | Main hall=Ichigensha {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} cypress bark roofing | Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Asukabe Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Asukabe-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall}}]] '''Asukabe Shrine''' (あすかべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Asuka, {{ill|lt=Habikino City|Q694308|qid=Q694308|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and under the {{ill|lt=modern system of shrine ranks|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined Deity === At present, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} is enshrined. This is because during the Edo period {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|qid=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} was the enshrined deity, and at the time of {{ill|lt=the separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|qid=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} it was changed to Susanoo-no-Mikoto. This area was the place of residence of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asukabe no Miyatsuko|1=UNKNOWN}} clan, descendants of {{ill|lt=Baekje|Q28428|qid=Q28428|1=Paekche}} royalty—specifically the descendants of {{ill|lt=King Gonji|Q6790050|qid=Q6790050|lt=King Gonji|1=Buyeo Gonji}}—who were dispatched and came to Japan in the {{ill|lt=5th century|Q8095|qid=Q8095|1=5th century}}. It is thought that originally King Gonji was enshrined as the ancestral deity of the Asukabe no Miyatsuko. In the Baekje Annals of the 『{{ill|lt=Samguk Sagi|Q485552|qid=Q485552|1=Samguk sagi}}』 it is recorded that King Gonji died in Baekje at the beginning of the Ungjin period; even if Gonji himself returned home, it is thought that his descendants remained in Japan. Nearby, the Shingū Kofun Cluster ({{ill|lt=horizontal stone chamber|Q11543435|qid=Q11543435|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) is said by one theory to be the burial ground of the Asuka clan. In 『Kawachi no Kuni Shikigami Shikō』 it is recorded as “Prince Yasuyado,” in 『Draft Manuscript of the Register of Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province』 as “Ancestral deity of the Baekje clan, commonly called {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}},” and in 『Jinja Yōroku』 as “Ancestral deity of the Baekje clan, name unknown.” On the other hand, {{ill|lt=Saeki Ariyoshi|Q13364959|qid=Q13364959|1=Saeki Ariyoshi}} states that in 『{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|qid=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}』, volume 24 (Ukyō Shoban) and volume 28 (Kawachi no Kuni Shoban), it is written: “{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Asukabe no Miyatsuko|1=UNKNOWN}}. Son of King Biu of Baekje, descended from {{ill|lt=King Gonji|Q6790050|qid=Q6790050|lt=King Gonji|1=Buyeo Gonji}}.”{{Cite book|Japanese book|author={{ill|lt=Saeki Ariyoshi|Q13364959|qid=Q13364959|1=Saeki Ariyoshi}}|date=1998-09-01|title=Studies on the Shinsen Shōjiroku|series=Textual Criticism Volume 5|publisher={{ill|lt=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Q4346755|qid=Q4346755|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan}}|ISBN=4642021159|page=2}} === History === The date of foundation is unknown. The first appearance in official histories is an entry dated August 13 of the first year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}) in 『{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}』, stating that the shrine was granted the divine rank of Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as “Asukabe Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasuka District|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|qid=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}},” and is ranked as a Myōjin Taisha. Until the Edo period, there was a {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|qid=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} (shrine-temple) called {{ill|lt=Jōrin-ji (Habikino City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Jōrin-ji}}, founded by {{ill|lt=Gyōki|Q1136664|qid=Q1136664|1=Gyōki}}, and it was designated a {{ill|lt=prayer temple|Q22131160|qid=Q22131160|1=Chokugansho}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|qid=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}. In the early Meiji period it was ranked as a Village Shrine, but in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 41 ({{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|qid=Q2056|1=1908}}) it was {{ill|lt=merged|Q11590700|qid=Q11590700|lt=merged|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} with a nearby Hachiman Shrine (now {{ill|lt=Tsuboi Hachimangū|Q11429677|qid=Q11429677|1=Tsuboi Hachimangū}}). In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 27 ({{ill|lt=1952|Q5272|qid=Q5272|1=1952}}) it was separated and rebuilt near the former shrine site. === Access === Get off at {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|Q901342|qid=Q901342|lt=Minami-Osaka Line|1=Minami Osaka Line}} {{ill|lt=Kaminotaishi Station|Q6358219|qid=Q6358219|1=Kaminotaishi Station}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|qid=Q1531085|1=Kintetsu Railway}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * {{commonscat-inline|Asukabe-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Asukabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Asukabe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Asukabe Shrine]][[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]]
